info.title: Mycelium JSON-RPC API
info.description: This is the specification of the Mycelium JSON-RPC API. It is used to perform admin tasks on the system, and to perform administrative duties.

servers.name: Mycelium JSON-RPC API (legacy TCP — kept for backwards compatibility)

methods.name: getInfo
methods.summary: Get general info about the node
methods.description: Get general info about the node, which is not related to other more specific functionality

result.name: info
result.description: General information about the node

methods.name: getPeers
methods.description: List all peers known in the system, and info about their connection. This includes the endpoint, how we know about the peer, the connection state, and if the connection is alive the amount of bytes we've sent to and received from the peer.

result.name: peers
result.description: List of peers

methods.name: addPeer
methods.description: Add a new peer identified by the provided endpoint. The peer is added to the list of known peers. It will eventually be connected to by the standard connection loop of the peer manager. This means that a peer which can't be connected to will stay in the system, as it might be reachable later on.

params.name: endpoint
params.description: The endpoint of the peer to add

result.name: success
result.description: Whether the peer was added successfully

methods.name: deletePeer
methods.description: Remove an existing peer identified by the provided endpoint. The peer is removed from the list of known peers. If a connection to it is currently active, it will be closed.

params.name: endpoint
params.description: The endpoint of the peer to remove

result.name: success
result.description: Whether the peer was removed successfully

methods.name: getSelectedRoutes
methods.description: List all selected routes in the system, and their next hop identifier, metric and sequence number. It is possible for a route to be selected and have an infinite metric. This route will however not forward packets.

result.name: routes
result.description: List of selected routes

methods.name: getFallbackRoutes
methods.description: List all fallback routes in the system, and their next hop identifier, metric and sequence number. These routes are available to be selected in case the selected route for a destination suddenly fails, or gets retracted.

result.name: routes
result.description: List of fallback routes

methods.name: getQueriedSubnets
methods.description: List all currently queried subnets in the system, and the amount of seconds until the query expires. These subnets are actively being probed in the network. If no route to them is discovered before the query expires, queued packets will be dropped and a new query can be started.

result.name: subnets
result.description: List of queried subnets

methods.name: popMessage
methods.summary: Get a message from the inbound message queue
methods.description: Get a message from the inbound message queue. By default, the message is removed from the queue and won't be shown again. If the peek parameter is set to true, the message will be peeked, and the next call to this method will show the same message. This method returns immediately by default: a message is returned if one is ready, and if there isn't nothing is returned. If the timeout parameter is set, this call won't return for the given amount of seconds, unless a message is received.

params.name: peek
params.description: Whether to peek the message or not. If this is true, the message won't be removed from the inbound queue when it is read

params.name: timeout
params.description: Amount of seconds to wait for a message to arrive if one is not available. Setting this to 0 is valid and will return a message if present, or return immediately if there isn't

params.name: topic
params.description: Optional filter for loading messages. If set, the system checks if the message has the given string at the start. This way a topic can be encoded.

result.name: message
result.description: The retrieved message, or null if no message was ready before the timeout expired

methods.name: pushMessage
methods.summary: Submit a new message to the system
methods.description: Push a new message to the systems outbound message queue. The system will continuously attempt to send the message until it is either fully transmitted, or the send deadline is expired. If `reply_timeout` is set and a reply arrives in time, the reply is returned; if it does not arrive in time, the message ID is returned (no error is raised).

params.name: message
params.description: The message to send

params.name: reply_timeout
params.description: Amount of seconds to wait for a reply to this message to come in. If not set, the system won't wait for a reply and return the ID of the message, which can be used later. If set, the system will wait for at most the given amount of seconds for a reply to come in. If a reply arrives, it is returned to the client. If not, the message ID is returned for later use.

result.name: response
result.description: The response to the message push

errors.message: Internal: reply subscription returned no value

methods.name: pushMessageReply
methods.summary: Reply to a message with the given ID
methods.description: Submits a reply message to the system, where ID is an id of a previously received message. If the sender is waiting for a reply, it will bypass the queue of open messages.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the message to reply to

params.name: message
params.description: The reply message

result.name: success
result.description: Whether the reply was submitted successfully

errors.message: Invalid message ID (must be 16 hex characters)

methods.name: getMessageInfo
methods.summary: Get the status of an outbound message
methods.description: Get information about the current state of an outbound message. This can be used to check the transmission state, size and destination of the message.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the message to get info for

result.name: info
result.description: Information about the message

errors.message: Invalid message ID (must be 16 hex characters)

methods.name: getPublicKeyFromIp
methods.summary: Get the pubkey from node ip
methods.description: Get the node's public key from it's IP address.

params.name: mycelium_ip
params.description: The IP address to get the public key for

result.name: pubkey
result.description: The hex encoded public key of the node

methods.name: getDefaultTopicAction
methods.description: Get the default action for topics that are not explicitly configured (accept or reject).

result.name: accept
result.description: Whether unconfigured topics are accepted by default

methods.name: setDefaultTopicAction
methods.description: Set the default action for topics that are not explicitly configured (accept or reject).

params.name: accept
params.description: Whether to accept unconfigured topics by default

result.name: success
result.description: Whether the default topic action was set successfully

methods.name: getTopics
methods.description: Get all topics that are explicitly configured in the system.

result.name: topics
result.description: List of configured topics

methods.name: addTopic
methods.description: Add a new topic to the system's whitelist.

params.name: topic
params.description: The topic to add

result.name: success
result.description: Whether the topic was added successfully

methods.name: removeTopic
methods.description: Remove a topic from the system's whitelist.

params.name: topic
params.description: The topic to remove

result.name: success
result.description: Whether the topic was removed successfully

methods.name: getTopicSources
methods.description: Get all sources (subnets) that are allowed to send messages for a specific topic.

params.name: topic
params.description: The topic to get sources for

result.name: sources
result.description: List of sources (subnets) for the topic

methods.name: addTopicSource
methods.summary: Add a source to a topic
methods.description: Add a source (subnet) that is allowed to send messages for a specific topic.

params.name: topic
params.description: The topic to add a source to

params.name: subnet
params.description: The subnet to add as a source

result.name: success
result.description: Whether the source was added successfully

methods.name: removeTopicSource
methods.summary: Remove a source from a topic
methods.description: Remove a source (subnet) that is allowed to send messages for a specific topic.

params.name: topic
params.description: The topic to remove a source from

params.name: subnet
params.description: The subnet to remove as a source

result.name: success
result.description: Whether the source was removed successfully

methods.name: getTopicForwardSocket
methods.summary: Get the forward socket for a topic
methods.description: Get the socket path where messages for a specific topic are forwarded to.

params.name: topic
params.description: The topic to get the forward socket for

result.name: socket_path
result.description: The socket path where messages are forwarded to

methods.name: setTopicForwardSocket
methods.summary: Set the forward socket for a topic
methods.description: Set the socket path where messages for a specific topic should be forwarded to.

params.name: topic
params.description: The topic to set the forward socket for

params.name: socket_path
params.description: The socket path where messages should be forwarded to

result.name: success
result.description: Whether the forward socket was set successfully

methods.name: removeTopicForwardSocket
methods.summary: Remove the forward socket for a topic
methods.description: Remove the socket path where messages for a specific topic are forwarded to.

params.name: topic
params.description: The topic to remove the forward socket for

result.name: success
result.description: Whether the forward socket was removed successfully

methods.name: getProxies
methods.description: List overlay IPv6 addresses of known proxies discovered by the node.

result.name: proxies
result.description: List of known proxies

methods.name: connectProxy
methods.description: Connect to a proxy. When no remote is provided, the node attempts to connect to the best known proxy. When a remote is provided, it attempts to connect to that address.

params.name: remote
params.description: Optional remote proxy socket address (host:port or [IPv6]:port)

result.name: socket
result.description: Connected proxy socket address

errors.message: No valid proxy available or connection failed

methods.name: disconnectProxy
methods.description: Disconnect from the currently connected proxy if any.

result.name: success
result.description: Whether the disconnect operation was triggered

methods.name: startProxyProbe
methods.description: Start scanning/probing for proxies on the network.

result.name: success
result.description: Whether probe was started

methods.name: stopProxyProbe
methods.description: Stop scanning/probing for proxies on the network.

result.name: success
result.description: Whether probe was stopped

methods.name: getPacketStatistics
methods.description: Get packet statistics grouped by source and destination IP addresses. Returns the number of packets and total bytes routed for each unique source IP and each unique destination IP separately.

result.name: statistics
result.description: Packet statistics grouped by source and destination

methods.name: network.getStatus
methods.description: Return a high-level snapshot of the host's network management state as seen by the mycelium daemon. Reports the platform, current listener policy, whether bridge and address management are supported on this platform, and counts of bridges, managed bridges, mycelium addresses (IPv6 in 400::/7) and currently active listeners.

result.name: status
result.description: Network management status

methods.name: network.listBridges
methods.summary: List bridges known to the host
methods.description: List Linux bridges discovered on the host. By default all bridges are returned; set `managed_only` to true to restrict the result to bridges created or tracked by mycelium. The `include_addresses` and `include_ports` flags control whether per-bridge address and port lists are populated.

params.name: managed_only
params.description: If true, only return bridges created or tracked by mycelium. Defaults to false.

params.name: include_addresses
params.description: If true, include the list of addresses on each bridge. Defaults to true.

params.name: include_ports
params.description: If true, include the list of enslaved ports on each bridge. Defaults to true.

result.name: bridges
result.description: List of bridges

methods.name: network.ensureBridge
methods.description: Ensure that a bridge with the given name exists on the host. If the bridge does not exist it is created; if it already exists, no error is raised. The `up` flag (default true) indicates whether the bridge should be brought up after creation. The `mtu` parameter sets the MTU for the bridge (default 1500). The response indicates whether the bridge was newly created.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the bridge to ensure

params.name: up
params.description: If true, bring the bridge up after ensuring it exists. Defaults to true.

params.name: mtu
params.description: MTU to set on the bridge. Defaults to 1500.

result.name: bridge
result.description: The ensured bridge and whether it was newly created

methods.name: network.deleteBridge
methods.description: Delete a Linux bridge by name. When `only_if_empty` is true (the default) the bridge is only deleted if it has no enslaved ports; otherwise the call returns an error.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the bridge to delete

params.name: only_if_empty
params.description: If true, only delete the bridge when it has no enslaved ports. Defaults to true.

result.name: result
result.description: Whether the bridge was deleted

methods.name: network.listAddresses
methods.description: List IP addresses visible to the mycelium daemon. By default only mycelium IPv6 addresses (in 400::/7) on bridges are returned. Use `interface` to restrict the listing to a single interface, and the `bridge_only`, `mycelium_only` and `managed_only` flags to narrow the scope further.

params.name: interface
params.description: If set, restrict the listing to this interface name. Null to return addresses across all interfaces.

params.name: bridge_only
params.description: If true, only include addresses on bridge interfaces. Defaults to true.

params.name: mycelium_only
params.description: If true, only include mycelium IPv6 addresses (in 400::/7). Defaults to true.

params.name: managed_only
params.description: If true, only include addresses managed by mycelium. Defaults to false.

result.name: addresses
result.description: List of addresses matching the filter

methods.name: network.addAddress
methods.summary: Add an address to an interface
methods.description: Add an IPv6 address to the given bridge interface and, optionally, activate a mycelium listener on it. Validation rules: this method is only supported on Linux; the target interface must exist and be a bridge; the address must be an IPv6 address in 400::/7; the address must not already be configured on the interface (no duplicates). When `activate_listener` is true (the default) the daemon attempts to (re)activate a listener on the new address according to the current listener policy.

params.name: interface
params.description: Name of the bridge interface to add the address to

params.name: address
params.description: IPv6 address to add (no prefix). Must lie in 400::/7.

params.name: prefix_len
params.description: Prefix length for the address. Defaults to 64.

params.name: activate_listener
params.description: If true, activate a mycelium listener on the new address. Defaults to true.

result.name: added
result.description: Details about the added address

methods.name: network.removeAddress
methods.summary: Remove an address from an interface
methods.description: Remove an IPv6 address from the given interface. If a mycelium listener is currently bound to the address it is torn down as part of the operation.

params.name: interface
params.description: Name of the interface to remove the address from

params.name: address
params.description: IPv6 address to remove (no prefix)

result.name: removed
result.description: Whether the address and any bound listener were removed

methods.name: network.getListeners
methods.description: Return the current listener policy and the list of mycelium listeners the daemon considers active. Each listener records the interface, address, port, source (how the listener was discovered), and whether it is currently bound.

result.name: listeners
result.description: Current listener policy and list of listeners

methods.name: network.setListenerPolicy
methods.description: Set the policy that controls which addresses the mycelium daemon binds listeners on. Supported policies are `all_mycelium_addresses` (bind every detected mycelium IPv6 address), `all_managed_addresses` (bind only mycelium-managed addresses), and `explicit_only` (bind only the addresses provided in `explicit_addresses`). When switching policy the daemon reloads its listener set and reports whether a reload occurred.

params.name: policy
params.description: The listener policy to apply

params.name: explicit_addresses
params.description: When policy is `explicit_only`, the list of addresses listeners should be bound to.

result.name: result
result.description: The applied policy and whether listeners were reloaded

nodeSubnet.description: The subnet owned by the node and advertised to peers

nodePubkey.description: The public key of the node

PacketStatEntry.description: Statistics for packets routed for a single IP address

PacketStatistics.description: Packet statistics grouped by source and destination IP

Endpoint.description: Identification to connect to a peer

type.description: How we know about this peer

connectionState.description: The current state of the connection to the peer

txBytes.description: The amount of bytes transmitted to this peer

rxBytes.description: The amount of bytes received from this peer

discovered.description: Number of seconds since the system first learned about this peer

lastConnected.description: Number of seconds since the last successful connection to this peer. Null if there has never been a successful connection

subnet.description: The overlay subnet for which this is the route

nextHop.description: A way to identify the next hop of the route, where forwarded packets will be sent

metric.description: The metric of the route, an estimation of how long the packet will take to arrive at its final destination

oneOf.description: An infinite (unreachable) metric. This is always `infinite`

seqno.description: the sequence number advertised with this route by the source

QueriedSubnet.description: Information about a subnet currently being queried

subnet.description: The overlay subnet which we are currently querying

expiration.description: The amount of seconds until the query expires

InboundMessage.description: A message received by the system

id.description: Id of the message, hex encoded

dstPk.description: Receiver public key, hex encoded. This is the public key of the system

payload.description: The message payload, encoded in standard alphabet base64

PushMessageBody.description: A message to send to a given receiver

payload.description: The message to send, base64 encoded

oneOf.description: An IP in the subnet of the receiver node

ip.description: The target IP of the message

oneOf.description: The hex encoded public key of the receiver node

pk.description: The hex encoded public key of the target node

PushMessageResponseId.description: The ID generated for a message after pushing it to the system

id.description: Id of the message, hex encoded

dst.description: IP address of the receiving node

created.description: Unix timestamp of when this message was created

deadline.description: Unix timestamp of when this message will expire. If the message is not received before this, the system will give up

msgLen.description: Length of the message in bytes

TransmissionState.description: The state of an outbound message in it's lifetime

source.description: How this listener was detected (kernel_address_scan, explicit, managed)
